# awscc_bedrock_enforced_guardrail_configuration (Resource)

Definition of AWS::Bedrock::EnforcedGuardrailConfiguration Resource Type



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `guardrail_identifier` (String) Identifier for the guardrail, could be the ID or the ARN
- `guardrail_version` (String) Numerical guardrail version (not DRAFT)

### Optional

- `model_enforcement` (Attributes) Model-specific information for the enforced guardrail configuration. If not present, the configuration is enforced on all models (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--model_enforcement))
- `selective_content_guarding` (Attributes) Selective content guarding controls for enforced guardrails (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--selective_content_guarding))

### Read-Only

- `config_id` (String) Unique ID for the account enforced configuration
- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the configuration was created
- `created_by` (String) The ARN of the role used to create the configuration
- `guardrail_arn` (String) ARN representation for the guardrail
- `guardrail_id` (String) Unique ID for the guardrail
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `owner` (String) Configuration owner type
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the configuration was last updated
- `updated_by` (String) The ARN of the role used to update the configuration

<a id="nestedatt--model_enforcement"></a>
### Nested Schema for `model_enforcement`

Optional:

- `excluded_models` (List of String) Models to exclude from enforcement. If a model is in both lists, it is excluded
- `included_models` (List of String) Models to enforce the guardrail on


<a id="nestedatt--selective_content_guarding"></a>
### Nested Schema for `selective_content_guarding`

Optional:

- `messages` (String) Selective guarding mode for user messages
- `system` (String) Selective guarding mode for system prompts

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_bedrock_enforced_guardrail_configuration.example
  identity = {
    config_id = "config_id"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `config_id` (String) Unique ID for the account enforced configuration

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_bedrock_enforced_guardrail_configuration.example
  id = "config_id"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_bedrock_enforced_guardrail_configuration.example "config_id"
```
